label: Therapeutic area
fhir_ccda_path: n/a — set to "Rare Cancers" at export
derivation_rule: Fixed value for this registry.

label: Who is reporting this case?
derivation_rule: Governance decision. A machine-derived case has no reporter until a human attests to it.

label: What disease did the participant receive treatment for?
derivation_rule: Requires a mapping from ICD-10-CM/SNOMED to the CURE ID controlled disease list. Not one to one; needs a published crosswalk.
cure_id_lossy: yes: ICD-10-CM / SNOMED specificity collapsed to the CURE ID disease list

label: Sarcoma subtype if not listed
derivation_rule: ICD-O-3 morphology is the right source and is rarely present in patient-directed FHIR. NLP over the pathology narrative, then human confirmation.
privacy_transform: free-text review before release; suppress below k threshold

label: Age at diagnosis (banded)
derivation_rule: Banded to the 18 CURE ID age groups (<1 month through 90+ years). Banding is also the privacy control.
cure_id_lossy: yes: exact age collapsed to band, by design

label: Sex assigned at birth
fhir_ccda_path: US Core Birth Sex extension; fallback Patient.gender
derivation_rule: Patient.gender is administrative gender, not sex assigned at birth. Where the extension is absent, ask on the survey rather than infer.

label: Country of treatment
derivation_rule: Defaults to United States; confirm on survey for participants with care abroad.

label: Race
derivation_rule: CURE ID uses OMB categories, so the value set aligns. EHR race is often incomplete or defaulted; the survey answer should win.
cure_id_lossy: yes: multi-race detail may collapse into OMB categories

label: Ethnicity
derivation_rule: Same precedence rule as race.

label: Predisposing risk factors
derivation_rule: Agent Orange, 9/11 exposure, environmental toxin exposure, prior radiation or chemotherapy. Effectively never structured in an EHR. This is the clearest argument for keeping the survey arm.

label: Tumor predisposition syndrome
derivation_rule: The comprehensive health survey carries this directly: a genetic testing module plus HPO-coded findings across 19 body systems. Outperforms the record for most participants.

label: Primary tumor location
derivation_rule: Maps cleanly to the 23-item CURE ID body-site list.
cure_id_lossy: yes: topography collapsed to a 23-item body-site list

label: Localized / locally advanced / metastatic
derivation_rule: Structured staging is uncommon in patient-directed pulls. Derive a first pass from secondary-malignancy codes, then confirm.
cure_id_lossy: yes: TNM detail collapsed to three categories

label: Metastatic site(s)
derivation_rule: Secondary neoplasm codes are well populated and map directly to the bone/brain/liver/lung/lymph-node list.

label: Year of diagnosis
derivation_rule: Year only. Do not export full dates.
privacy_transform: year and month only; no day
cure_id_lossy: yes: day and month precision removed

label: Time from first encounter to diagnosis
derivation_rule: The record estimate is systematically short because it misses care before the connected system. The health survey captures symptom onset and diagnosis age as separate items, which yields the interval directly.

label: Reason for diagnostic delay
derivation_rule: Free text. High narrative value; no structured analogue.

label: Signs and symptoms at diagnosis
derivation_rule: The health survey records each finding as an HPO term with age of first symptom captured separately from age of diagnosis, which is more structured than the equivalent record data rather than less.

label: Biopsy or resection confirmed
fhir_ccda_path: Procedure (SNOMED biopsy) or DiagnosticReport surgical pathology
derivation_rule: Presence of a surgical pathology report is strong evidence.

label: Expert pathology review
derivation_rule: Reviewer expertise is not a coded attribute anywhere.

label: Genomic testing performed
derivation_rule: Presence or absence of the report answers the yes/no reliably.

label: Somatic or germline
derivation_rule: Usually inferable from the specimen and report header; confirm before export.

label: Molecular abnormality detected
derivation_rule: Structured where a genomics vendor feed exists; otherwise NLP over the report. The health survey also asks whether the participant holds a copy of the report and supports upload, which is often the faster route.
privacy_transform: free-text review before release; suppress below k threshold

label: Discussed at tumor board
derivation_rule: Occasionally in notes; not reliably.

label: Previously misdiagnosed
derivation_rule: Problem-list churn is a useful prompt for the participant, not an answer on its own.

label: Radiation / surgery / systemic therapy
derivation_rule: The three modality flags are well supported.

label: Best surgical outcome
derivation_rule: Margin status lives in the pathology narrative. NLP plus confirmation.

label: Systemic drugs administered
derivation_rule: The core of the submission. Walk every translation child of the coding so an inner RxNorm code is not lost inside an outer NDC.
cure_id_lossy: partial: product-level detail collapsed to ingredient

label: Treatment start
derivation_rule: Exported as year and month only.
privacy_transform: year and month only; no day

label: Still taking the drug
fhir_ccda_path: MedicationStatement.status; last dispense plus days supply
derivation_rule: A stale record looks identical to an ongoing one. Ask.

label: Treatment end
fhir_ccda_path: last dispense + days supply, or effectivePeriod.end
derivation_rule: Discontinuation is usually implicit. Participant confirmation matters most here.
privacy_transform: year and month only; no day

label: Dose
derivation_rule: Frequently only a free-text sig. Parse, then show for confirmation.
cure_id_lossy: partial: dose schedule collapsed to a single reported dose

label: Frequency
derivation_rule: Same caveat as dose.

label: Route of administration
derivation_rule: Usually coded.

label: Significant adverse event
fhir_ccda_path: Condition / AllergyIntolerance / Observation in temporal proximity
derivation_rule: Temporal proximity is a signal, not an attribution. Never auto-assert causality; put it to the participant or clinician.

label: Which adverse events
fhir_ccda_path: Condition.code; map to the CURE ID adverse-event list
derivation_rule: PRO-CTCAE items already in this registry give a stronger, participant-sourced answer, and HPO-coded survey findings supply the pre-existing phenotype needed to separate a new event from a long-standing one.

label: Reason stopped
derivation_rule: Adverse event, progression, participant choice, withdrawal of consent. Intent is not recorded in the EHR.

label: On-label / off-label SOC / novel use
fhir_ccda_path: RxNorm ingredient compared with FDA labeled indication (SPL/DailyMed) against Condition.code
derivation_rule: The pivotal computation of the whole project. Automate the first pass, require human adjudication, and keep the reasoning auditable.

label: In what new way was it used
fhir_ccda_path: compare dose, route, schedule, and combination against label
derivation_rule: New indication versus new dose, schedule, duration, route, combination, or population. Partly computable, partly judgment.

label: Goals of the line of therapy
derivation_rule: Cure, control, quality of life, symptom relief, prolonged life. Intent is not a coded field.

label: Best outcome on this line
derivation_rule: RECIST response is almost never structured in patient-directed data. Expect this to come from the survey plus radiology-report NLP.
cure_id_lossy: yes: response trajectory collapsed to a single best outcome

label: Duration of response
fhir_ccda_path: interval between response and progression observations
derivation_rule: Derivable only where response is structured, which is rare.

label: Outcome ongoing
derivation_rule: Point-in-time participant report.

label: Other therapy details
derivation_rule: Free text.

label: Outcome at time of report
derivation_rule: Loss of life is the one outcome where an external linkage (National Death Index, or a tokenized mortality file) materially improves ascertainment over the portal record.
cure_id_lossy: yes: point-in-time status only; no longitudinal course

label: Additional information
derivation_rule: Free text narrative.

label: Upload images or reports
derivation_rule: Participant-directed C-CDA already contains documents; offer them for selective attachment rather than asking for a re-upload.
